lines.label: Subject
lines.detail: levels=18, rows=180, obs_per_level=10..10, basis=2, cov_params=3, levels/basis=9.00, levels/param=6.00, rows/param=60.00; grouping levels are the effective n for covariance support; risk=v0 information budget is sufficient; recommendation=information budget is sufficient under v0 thresholds; explanation=180 rows are clustered into 18 grouping levels; grouping levels are the effective n for random-effect distribution support

lines.label: Subject
lines.detail: `Subject` units differ in baseline and `Days` slope; the model estimates whether these are associated. It uses 3 covariance parameters for intercept, Days. The data contain 18 `Subject` levels, with at least 10 rows per level (median 10). Within-group support is present for `Days`. Within-group support was not assessed for `intercept`. The grouping-level information is sufficient under the current audit thresholds. Formula detail: `(1 + Days | Subject)`.

lines.label: constraints
lines.detail: none

lines.label: kernels
lines.detail: Observations sharing `Subject` are linked through `intercept`, `Days`, with full covariance (marginal path).

lines.label: repeated units
lines.detail: `Subject` has 18 levels and 10..10 rows per level; its marginal path is covered by one requested random-effect term.

lines.label: missing paths
lines.detail: none

lines.label: Subject
lines.detail: source=(1 + Days | Subject); group=Subject; family=FullCholesky; user_basis=intercept, Days; optimizer_basis=intercept, Days; theta_slots=theta[0], theta[1], theta[2]; lambda_slots=(0, 0), (1, 0), (1, 1); parmap_aligned=3/3; varcorr_entries=sd(intercept), sd(Days), corr(Days,intercept)
